module1.clean_text:
[ID] P: BAGHDAD [PATIENT] TASNIME
Born on [DATE] [PATIENT] No: [ID] | Date [DATE] | Date Entry: [DATE] Date: [DATE] P: [PATIENT] TASNIME
Born on [DATE] [PATIENT] No: [ID] | Resident: DR.TAMAZIRT | Assistant: Dr OUSSALAH
RE-HOSPITALIZATION FORM
M.O. HABIB
Family history:
Father: [AGE] years old, asthmatic
Mother: [AGE] years old, in good health
Siblings: The innate of a sibling of [AGE] Brother suffering from cystic fibrosis monitored at our level:
Baghdad [PATIENT] born in [DATE] File No. [ID] history:
Physiological:
Well-monitored pregnancy
Full-term birth, PN: 4kg300
Meconium emitted < 24hrs
Mixed breastfeeding since birth
Dietary diversification at the age of [AGE] to date vaccinations
Good psychomotor development
Pathological:
Cystic fibrosis with dual digestive and respiratory expression since the age of [AGE] of the disease
Primary diagnosis: Cystic fibrosis
Date of diagnosis: [DATE] of diagnosis: [AGE] elements:
Anamnestic:
Consanguinity: 3rd degree
Clinic:
Recurrent hacking cough since the age of [AGE] and a half [AGE] with bronchial congestion
Abdominal bloating from birth
Chronic diarrhea since the age of [AGE] twice for severe DSH with hyponatremia
Severe malnutrition P/T (Waterloo) =76%. Paraclinic:
1st sweat test: sweat level: 165mg/ chlorine level: 119meq/l
2nd sweat test: sweat level: 220mg/chlorine level: 130 meq/l
Fecal elastase: 0.2µg/g
Genetic study: Classic form of cystic fibrosis, homozygous variant c579+1G>T. Processing in progress:
- For respiratory purposes:
Respiratory physiotherapy at home twice daily
-For nutritional purposes:
Substitution opotherapy: Creon 12000 IU 08 capsules per day, i.e.: 4800 IU/day
Alvityl 1 tsp/d
Vit K 10 mg/15 days
Vit D amp/2 months
Vit E 1cp/d
Therapeutic compliance: Good
Events since the last visit:
Hospitalized for exacerbation and clarification on 07/31/2025
Consultation: No
Respiratory: intermittent productive cough, whitish sputum, eupneic
On the digestive level: Number of stools 1-2 stools/day, preserved transit, preserved appetite. Nutritionally: good nutritional status. On the ENT level: RAS. Activity: yes. Schooling: YES in preschool. Reason for hospitalization this episode: Exacerbation of his illness. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 11/18/2025
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 20 kg [+0.53DS]
Size: 102cm [-0.3 DS]
BMI: 17 (+1.15DS) Good nutritional status
GOMEZ: 110%
WATERLOO: 100%
General condition good, conscious cooperating, T: 36.5°C, TCNC
ENT examination:
Clean throat
No cervical lymphadenopathy, the rest without particularities. Cardiovascular examination:
HR: 105b/m, B1 B2 audible. No hiss or added noises. Respiratory symptoms:
FR:25c/m SaO2:98% under ambient air
Lung auscultation: clear. Intermittent cough, no respiratory distress, no clubbing. Digestive/nutritional symptoms:
Abdomen bloated and supple on palpation. No collateral circulation, no organomegaly
Stools of normal consistency 1 to 2 stools/day (oily sometimes)
No GERD or rectal prolapse
Neurological examination:
Conscious patient 15/15 on the Glasgow score. No motor or sensory problems. Examination of the skin and appendages:
Standard colored integuments
No damage to the scalp or skin appendages. Osteoarticular examination:
No limitation of movements
Support:
Multiple daily physiotherapy
Biantibiotherapy according to the antibiogram
Blood tests and ECBC
Interview with dietitian
Interview with psychologist
Interview with respiratory physiotherapy
Monitoring
Complete the development and impact of your illness
Evolution:
On day 02:
Complete assessment has been launched:
Hematology:
Hb: 13.7g/dl Leukocytes: 9700 /mm³. Platelets: 313,000/mm³.
CRP: negative PCT: Negative
Biochemistry:
Na+: 133 mmol/L Cl⁻: 107.4 mmol/L K+: 4.01 mmol/L
ASAT/ALAT: 30.7/26.2 IU/L
Urea/creat: 0.25/5.97
Ferritinemia: 12.52 ng/ml
Gamma GT: 16.09 PAL: 267
Vitamin D: 20ng/l
CAT: Vitamin D Supplementation
On day 03:
Dietary maintenance: Good nutritional status
Maintenance and updating of physiotherapy with the mother: Effective and correct physiotherapy technique
ECBC Results:
Sputum culture (20-11-2025): bacterial: Ralstonia Pickettii sensitive to fortum. So we continued the same antibiotic therapy. Yeasts and mushrooms: Negative. During her hospitalization which lasted 15 days, she received 14 days of Fortum and 10 days of Amikacin with a good clinical evolution, reduction in the consistency of the sputum; outgoing on 02-12-2025 with control appointment at the specialized consultation with influenza vaccine. Development and impact of his illness:
On the respiratory level:
Clinically:
No chest deformity
No digital clubbing
Paraclinic:
Chest x-ray: unremarkable
Thoracic CT (08/20/2025): slight chest distension
Gait test: normal. Aspergillus serology (08-21-2025): Negative
Arterial blood gas analysis requested, not yet done
Microbiology:
Sputum culture (20-11-2025): bacterial: Ralstonia Pickettii
Yeasts and mushrooms: Negative. On the cardiac level:
Clinically: B1 B2 audible. No breath or added noise
Cardiac ultrasound (12-08-2025): LV EF: 60%, NO dilation or hypertrophy of the cavities, normal right pressure 15 mmHg (absence of PAH). On the ENT level:
No nasal obstruction or rhinorrhea. No smell problem. On the digestive level:
Clinic:
Abdomen bloated and supple on palpation. No collateral circulation, no organomegaly
Stools of normal consistency 1 to 2 stools/day (oily sometimes)
Paraclinic:
Abdomino-pelvic ultrasound (01-09-2025): Unremarkable. Digestive and pancreatic assessment: Fecal elastase: 0.2µg/g
Nutritionally:
Clinic:
No failure to thrive
Good nutritional status
Paraclinic:
Vitamin K: (TP = 81.6%)
Cholesterol: 1.52 correct
Triglycerides: 0.40g/l correct
high-calorie supplements
ADEK supplementation
on a psychological level:
Supportive psychotherapy. CONCLUSION:
This is the child BAGHDAD MALAK TASSNIM aged 05 years old from MOSTAGANEM and residing in ORAN, from a 3rd degree consanguineous couple followed in our department since the age of 4 months for Cystic fibrosis with dual respiratory and digestive expression on the phenotypic level and of homozygous genetic type for the variant c579+1G>T. admitted to our department on 11/18/2025 for exacerbation of his illness with good progress. Release order:
Prescription for flu vaccine
Respiratory physiotherapy
Substitution opotherapy: Creon 12000 IU 08 capsules per day, i.e.: 4800 IU/day
Alvityl 1 tsp/d
Vit K 10 mg/15 days
Vit D amp/2 months
Vit E 1cp/d
Please recover:
Arterial blood gas results. Resident: Dr. Tamazirt Assistant: Dr. Oussalah. M.O. HABIB
